summary: Tested backend (35 pytest cases) and frontend critical flows for Inventory Management Platform. Found and FIXED 3 critical bugs (2 backend, 1 frontend) that blocked core functionality. All 35 backend tests pass after fixes. UI flows verified end-to-end: admin login → dashboard → products CRUD → locations → stock ledger → logout, plus worker register → /worker route.

critical.issue: FIXED. Returned 500 ResponseValidationError - motor's insert_one mutates user_doc to add ObjectId _id, which overrode the str user_id in `{'_id': user_id, **user_doc}`. Fixed by popping _id from user_doc before merge.
FIXED. Returned 500 KeyError: 'id' - code used `current_user['id']` but get_current_user returns dict with key `_id` (not `id`). Fixed by using `current_user['_id']`.

minor.issue: Cookies set with secure=False; acceptable for preview env but should be True in production (samesite='lax' with secure=True for cross-site cookies).
Returns full user dict with raw `_id` string instead of clean `UserResponse` model. Works but inconsistent with /api/auth/me shape (frontend uses `user.role` so functional).
No lockout after N failed login attempts (playbook recommends 5-fail lockout). Not implemented.
Threshold check uses `$lt` so a product whose stock equals threshold is NOT flagged. Likely intended `<=`. Verify business intent with main agent.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED. Used `<SignOut>` icon which was never imported (only `LogOut` is in scope). Would crash the page with ReferenceError as soon as the header rendered. Replaced all four occurrences with `<LogOut>`.

issues: Radix Dialog component throws a11y warning: Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby` on DialogContent — add DialogDescription for accessibility.

action_items: Verify low-stock threshold operator: should it be `<` or `<=` (current `<` excludes exact-match cases).
Consider standardizing user response shape: /api/auth/login should return UserResponse like other endpoints (currently returns raw mongo doc with string _id).
Add DialogDescription to all shadcn Dialogs (Products/Locations/Worker) to remove a11y warnings.
Implement brute-force lockout (5 failed logins) per auth playbook recommendation.
Set secure=True on auth cookies in production environments.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 522 lines - acceptable but borderline; would benefit from splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/products.py, routers/stock.py once it grows further.
Same MongoDB aggregation pipeline for computing current_stock is duplicated 3 times (create_product, get_products, get_dashboard_stats, get_low_stock_alerts). Extract into helper.
register/login both manually set cookies inline; extract into a `set_auth_cookies(response, user_id)` helper.
get_current_user uses ObjectId(payload['sub']) but doesn't catch InvalidId - if token has malformed sub, this raises 500 instead of 401.
No rate limiting on /api/auth/login - vulnerable to credential stuffing.
All API endpoints sit on api_router with no per-endpoint auth dependency declaration audit. Sync endpoints accept any authenticated user (admin or worker) - intentional but document it.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (fixed register response and stock transaction user_id key)
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed products/locations and unique-email worker accounts each run; no global seed required (admin seeded on backend startup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers auth, products CRUD + RBAC, locations CRUD + RBAC, immutable stock ledger, dashboard stats/low-stock, sync push/pull. Run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Key fixed bugs: (1) register endpoint ObjectId leak via dict spread, (2) stock transaction used wrong user key, (3) SignOut icon used without import on 4 pages. If main agent adds new endpoints, extend backend_test.py classes. Frontend uses data-testid extensively; Login uses login-email-input/login-password-input/login-submit-button, nav uses nav-dashboard/nav-products/nav-locations/nav-ledger, Products form uses product-{sku,name,description,threshold,unit}-input and product-submit-button.
rca of the issue: (1) Register 500: motor's AsyncIOMotorCollection.insert_one mutates the input dict in-place by adding `_id` (ObjectId). Code then did `{'_id': str_id, **user_doc}` where **user_doc spread re-introduced the ObjectId, and Pydantic's str-typed _id field rejected ObjectId. Fix: pop _id before spreading. (2) Stock transaction 500: get_current_user returns user dict with `_id` (string) but stock_transaction and sync_push referenced `current_user['id']` — KeyError. Fix: use `_id`. (3) Frontend pages crashed on render: developer renamed `LogOut` to `SignOut` in JSX but forgot to update the import; React threw ReferenceError. Fix: revert to LogOut in 4 files.
